steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $9x + 36$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Apply the distributive property
steps.content: Multiply 9 by each term inside the parentheses: $$9(x + 4) = 9 \cdot x + 9 \cdot 4 = 9x + 36$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• A: $9x + 4$ — Only the $x$ was multiplied by 9; the constant 4 was left unchanged. Eliminated.
  • B: $9x + 13$ — The 4 was added to 9 rather than multiplied. Eliminated.
  • C: $9x + 5$ — No correct distributive step applied to $x + 4$ produces a constant of 5. Eliminated.
  • D: $9x + 36$ — Both terms correctly multiplied by 9. That is the correct answer.
